zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Ferrers於大約1248年出生在英國戴文易爾福拉可姆時，她的父親Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby57歲，母親Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby31歲。她於大約1267年與Thomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeley在英國結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1309年3月19日在英國英格蘭布里斯托享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭布里斯托。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Ferrers于大约1248年出生在英国戴文易爾福拉可姆时，她的父亲Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby57岁，母亲Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby31岁。她于大约1267年与Thomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeley在英国结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1309年3月19日在英国英格兰布里斯托享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰布里斯托。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ferrers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire (Eure) and Ferrières (Manche).

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferridge
meanings: English: unexplained; possibly a form of Ferris and therefore a variant of Ferrers .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Ferrers was born about 1248, in Ilfracombe, Devon, England, her father, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, was 57 and her mother, Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby, was 31. She married Thomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeley about 1267, in England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 19 March 1309, in Bristol, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Bristol, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Ferrers est née environ 1248, à Ilfracombe, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, avait 57 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeley environ 1267, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 19 mars 1309, à Bristol, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Bristol, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Ferrers um 1248 in Ilfracombe, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby 57 und ihre Mutter Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby 31. Sie hat um 1267 in England Thomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 19. März 1309 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Bristol, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Bristol, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Ferrers è nata il circa 1248 nel Ilfracombe, Devon, England, sua padre, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, aveva 57 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Thomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeley circa 1267, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 19 marzo 1309, nel Bristol, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a Bristol, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Ferrersが約1248年にイギリスデボンイルフラコンベで生まれた時，父Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derbyは57歳，母Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derbyは31歳であった。約1267年にイギリスでThomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1309年3月19日にイギリスイギリスブリストルで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスブリストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Ferrers(이)가 대략 1248년 영국 데폰 이프라코브에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby(은)는 57세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 대략 1267년 영국에서 Thomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1309년 3월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 브리스톨에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 브리스톨에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Ferrers nasceu aproximadamente 1248, em Ilfracombe, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, tinha 57 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeley aproximadamente 1267, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 19 de março de 1309, em Bristol, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Bristol, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Ferrers родилась около 1248 г. в местности илфракомб, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby было 57 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeley около 1267 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 19 марта 1309 г. в местности бристол, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности бристол, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Ferrers nació aproximadamente 1248, en Ilfracombe, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, tenía 57 años y su mamá, Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Thomas de Berkeley 1st Baron Berkeley aproximadamente 1267, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 19 de marzo de 1309, en Bristol, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Bristol, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
